title: Kai and the Case of the Missing Rainbow
synopsis: When the rainbow disappears from Sunny Park, young detective Kai teams up with his friends Maya and Zoe to solve the mystery using science and observation skills.

pages.text: Kai loved rainbows. Every time it rained, he would run outside. He would look up at the sky with his magnifying glass. "Where is the rainbow?" Kai would ask. Today was different. It had just rained. The sun was shining bright. But there was no rainbow in Sunny Park. "This is strange," said Kai. He put on his detective hat. "I need to solve this mystery!"

illustration.description: A young Asian boy with short black hair wearing a yellow detective hat and holding a magnifying glass, looking up at a bright blue sky with white clouds. He's standing in a colorful park with green grass, purple flowers, and a playground in the background. His expression shows curiosity and determination.
illustration.alt: Kai looking up at the sky in Sunny Park wearing his detective hat

pages.text: Kai ran to find his friends. Maya was reading under the big oak tree. She had curly brown hair and kind eyes. "Maya!" called Kai. "The rainbow is missing!" Maya looked up from her book. "Missing? That's impossible," she said. "Rainbows can't just disappear." "But it did!" said Kai. "Will you help me find it?" Maya closed her book. "Of course! This sounds like a real mystery."

illustration.description: A girl with curly brown hair and dark skin sitting under a large oak tree with green leaves, holding an open book. She looks surprised and interested. Kai is running toward her with his arms outstretched, still wearing his yellow detective hat. The park setting has warm golden sunlight filtering through the tree branches.
illustration.alt: Maya reading under the oak tree as Kai runs to ask for help

pages.text: Next, they found Zoe by the fountain. She was drawing with colorful chalk. Zoe had red pigtails and freckles. "Zoe!" called Maya. "We need your help!" Zoe looked up. Her hands were covered in rainbow chalk dust. "What's wrong?" she asked. "The rainbow is missing from the park," said Kai. "We're going to find it!" Zoe jumped up. "Count me in! I love rainbows too." She wiped her chalky hands on her shirt. "Let's go detect!"

illustration.description: A girl with red pigtails and fair skin with freckles, kneeling by a stone fountain with colorful chalk scattered around her. Her hands are dusty with rainbow-colored chalk. Maya and Kai are approaching her, with the fountain spraying water in the background. The scene is bright and cheerful with blue water and colorful chalk art on the ground.
illustration.alt: Zoe drawing with chalk by the fountain as Maya and Kai approach

pages.text: The three friends walked around the park. Kai held his magnifying glass high. "Let's look for clues," he said. "Where do rainbows come from?" asked Zoe. Maya thought hard. "My mom told me rainbows need sunlight and water," she said. "Like rain drops!" Kai nodded. "Good thinking! We have sunlight." He pointed up at the bright sun. "But where are the water drops?"

illustration.description: Three children walking together in a sunny park. Kai holds a magnifying glass up to his eye, Maya is pointing thoughtfully at the sky, and Zoe is looking around curiously. The sun is bright yellow in a blue sky, casting long shadows on the green grass. Flowers and trees surround them in vibrant colors.
illustration.alt: The three friends searching for clues in the sunny park

pages.text: They searched everywhere. Behind the big slide. Under the monkey bars. Around the flower garden. "No water drops anywhere," said Maya. Zoe sat down on a bench. "Wait!" she said. "What about the sprinklers?" She pointed to the park sprinklers. "They make water drops!" Kai's eyes got wide. "Brilliant! But why aren't they working?" The sprinklers were completely dry. Not one drop of water came out.

illustration.description: The three children examining broken sprinklers in the park. The sprinklers are tall and metallic, but no water is coming out. Zoe is pointing at them excitedly, while Kai peers through his magnifying glass at the sprinkler head. Maya is crouched down looking at the base. The playground equipment is visible in the background with bright primary colors.
illustration.alt: The friends discovering the broken sprinklers

pages.text: "The sprinklers are broken!" said Kai. "But who can fix them?" Maya snapped her fingers. "Mr. Carlos! He takes care of the park." They ran to find Mr. Carlos. He was planting flowers by the entrance. Mr. Carlos had gray hair and a big smile. "Hello, detectives!" he said. "What brings you here?" "The sprinklers are broken," said Zoe. "And we can't find the rainbow!" Mr. Carlos scratched his head. "Oh my! I forgot to turn them on this morning!"

illustration.description: An older Latino man with gray hair and a kind smile, wearing a green park uniform and gardening gloves, kneeling by colorful flower beds near the park entrance. The three children are running toward him excitedly. He's holding a small watering can and has dirt on his knees. Bright red, yellow, and purple flowers surround him.
illustration.alt: Mr. Carlos the park keeper planting flowers as the children approach

pages.text: Mr. Carlos led them to a small shed. Inside was a big control panel with lots of buttons. "This controls all the sprinklers," he explained. "I was so busy with the flowers, I forgot!" He pressed a large red button. CLICK! Suddenly, water began spraying everywhere. "Hooray!" cheered the children. The sprinklers made tiny water droplets that sparkled in the sunlight. "Now we need to wait," said Kai wisely.

illustration.description: Inside a small wooden shed with Mr. Carlos pressing a large red button on a control panel filled with colorful switches and lights. Water can be seen spraying outside through the open door. The children are watching excitedly, with Kai still wearing his detective hat. The control panel glows with green and blue lights, creating a magical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Mr. Carlos turning on the sprinklers from the control shed

pages.text: They ran back outside. The sprinklers were working perfectly! Water droplets danced in the air. The sun shone through each tiny drop. "Look!" shouted Maya. "I see colors!" Sure enough, a beautiful rainbow was forming. Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple stripes appeared across the sky. "We solved the mystery!" said Kai proudly. "The rainbow wasn't really missing. It just needed sunlight AND water drops."

illustration.description: A magnificent rainbow arcing across the bright blue sky above the park, with water droplets sparkling in the sunlight from multiple sprinklers. The three children are jumping with joy and pointing up at the rainbow. The scene is filled with vibrant colors - the rainbow's stripes are bold and bright, the grass is emerald green, and flowers bloom in every color.
illustration.alt: The beautiful rainbow appearing over the park with the children celebrating

pages.text: Zoe clapped her hands. "Science is amazing!" she said. Maya nodded. "Sunlight plus water drops equals rainbow!" Mr. Carlos smiled. "You three are excellent detectives," he said. "Thank you for reminding me about the sprinklers." Kai took off his detective hat. "Case closed!" he announced. They all sat on the grass and watched the rainbow. It was the most beautiful rainbow they had ever seen. And they had brought it back to Sunny Park together.

illustration.description: All four characters sitting on lush green grass looking up at the rainbow together. Mr. Carlos sits with the three children, all smiling peacefully. The rainbow stretches majestically overhead while sprinklers continue to create sparkly mist around them. The park is alive with color - flowers, trees, and playground equipment all bathed in the rainbow's magical light.
illustration.alt: The group sitting together on the grass admiring the rainbow they helped create

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was Kai looking for in the park?
questions.explanation: Kai was looking for the rainbow that was missing from Sunny Park after it rained.

questions.question: What two things do you need to make a rainbow?
questions.explanation: Maya remembered that rainbows need both sunlight and water drops to form.

questions.question: Who takes care of Sunny Park?
questions.explanation: Mr. Carlos is the park keeper who was planting flowers and forgot to turn on the sprinklers.

questions.question: Why were the sprinklers not working?
choices: Mr. Carlos forgot to turn them on
questions.explanation: Mr. Carlos was busy planting flowers and forgot to turn on the sprinklers that morning.

questions.question: How did the children solve the mystery?
choices: They worked together and used science
questions.explanation: The children worked as a team and used their knowledge of science to figure out that rainbows need both sunlight and water drops.

pages.title: Rainbow Science
pages.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete the rainbow facts!
pages.text: To make a rainbow, you need ____ and ____. When sunlight shines through tiny ____, it creates beautiful colors. The colors of the rainbow are red, orange, ____, green, blue, and purple.

pages.title: Detective Tools
pages.instructions: Help Kai organize his detective supplies!
pages.text: Every good detective needs special ____. Kai uses a ____ glass to look at clues closely. He wears a ____ hat so everyone knows he's a detective. When he solves a mystery, he says '____!'
